summary: Imported miscellaneous tooling and repo-quality-of-life changes from PR #766. The main addition was a new Rust workspace tool, tools/replace-module, for replacing anonymous func_/lbl_XXXXXXXX symbols with a module prefix across source files. The PR also tightened shell helper scripts, shared root-directory logic through melee-utils in tools/issues, adjusted compile_flags.txt with -fno-short-enums, cleaned redundant lock/requirements files, and updated the project glossary with decomp naming abbreviations.
what_changed: Added tools/replace-module as a new Rust workspace member named melee-replace-module with AGPL-3.0-or-later licensing and dependencies on clap, regex, walkdir, globset, itertools, env_logger, log, anyhow, lazy_static, and melee-utils.
Implemented replace-module to accept a prefix and an optional symbols path; if the symbols argument is absent or '-', it reads symbols from stdin.

replace-module extracts unique func_/lbl_XXXXXXXX addresses from the supplied symbols text, builds a regex matching those anonymous symbols, and replaces them across paths returned by melee_utils::walk_src with prefix_$1.
Added -fno-short-enums to compile_flags.txt, affecting tools that consume the compile flags for clang parsing.
Updated docs/glossary.md with abbreviations deg, lr, mv, and rad; removed the previous SV glossary entry.

Updated tools/functions.sh with a melee_sed helper, safer quoting/read usage, rename_tu helper for renaming translation units across asm/src/docs, updated gen_header to include <platform.h>, and exported WINE=wibo.
Changed replace_symbol in tools/functions.sh to cover asm, src, and obj_files.mk and use a regex matching source-like extensions.
Updated tools/issues from version 0.1.0 to 0.2.0, added a melee-utils dependency, and replaced a local set_current_dir implementation with melee_utils::set_current_dir.
Simplified anyhow context calls in tools/issues from with_context closures to context where no dynamic formatting was needed.
Renamed the tools/flags CLI struct from Cli to Args and replaced magic numeric bounds with named constants I32_MAX, U32_MAX, and I64_MAX.
Made tools/newlines.sh stricter with set -euo pipefail and guarded tail/append logic with an existence check.
Removed tools/dadosod/Cargo.lock, implying dadosod should rely on the top-level workspace lock rather than a nested lockfile.
Removed unused dadosod variables/helpers, including is_load_store in tracker.rs and is_aligned in utils.rs.
Adjusted tools/vimrc colorcolumn from 81 to 80 and added .clang-format YAML filetype/syntax handling.
smart_moves: Promoted repeated project-root and source-walking behavior into melee-utils users instead of duplicating set_current_dir logic in individual tools.
Made replace-module accept either a file or stdin, which fits shell pipelines from disassembly, symbol lists, or grep/sed output.
Deduplicated extracted anonymous symbol addresses with itertools::unique before building the replacement regex, avoiding redundant alternation entries.
Used word-boundary regex matching for func_/lbl_XXXXXXXX replacement, reducing the risk of partial symbol substitutions.
Added a dedicated Rust tool for module-wide anonymous symbol replacement while retaining lighter shell helpers for quick rename workflows.
Added -fno-short-enums to the shared compile flags so clang-based tooling sees enum sizing closer to the project's target assumptions.
Improved shell-script robustness with quoting, read -r, set -euo pipefail, and file-existence checks.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a decompiled translation unit or module, update source, asm, docs, obj_files.mk, and build-script references together; tools/functions.sh and replace-module encode that workflow.
Anonymous symbols of the form func_XXXXXXXX and lbl_XXXXXXXX can be mechanically converted to module-prefixed names when the relevant address list is known.
Shared compile_flags.txt changes affect clang-based tooling, so target ABI details such as enum size should live there rather than being handled ad hoc per tool.
Small CLI tools should share repo traversal and root-directory helpers through melee-utils to avoid each tool making slightly different assumptions about project layout.
Keep Rust tools in the top-level Cargo workspace and top-level Cargo.lock unless there is a clear reason for a nested lockfile.
Glossary entries are useful for stabilizing abbreviation meaning across decomp code; this PR documents deg, rad, lr, and mv as accepted terms.
naming_conventions: Use Args as the clap parser struct name in small Rust CLI tools rather than Cli, as shown by tools/flags and the new replace-module tool.
Anonymous assembly/decomp symbols follow func_XXXXXXXX and lbl_XXXXXXXX, where XXXXXXXX is an 8-digit hexadecimal address.
Glossary: lr means left/right and is synonymous with facing direction.
Glossary: mv stands for motion variables, used by HSD_GObj::user_data structs such as Fighter::mv to store polymorphic variables specific to a MotionState.
The previous glossary entry for SV / State Variables was removed in this diff; the PR does not explain the rationale beyond the replacement glossary edits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: tools/replace-module scans input text with Regex::new(r"(?:lbl|func)_([[:xdigit:]]{8})") to collect target addresses.
replace-module builds a second regex r"\b(?:lbl|func)_(addr1|addr2|...)\b" and replaces matches with prefix_$1 across walked source files.
tools/functions.sh rename_module_symbols_asm extracts lines matching ^(func|lbl)_([0-9A-F]{8})\b:$ from asm labels and maps them to a module prefix.
tools/functions.sh rename_module_symbols_c extracts function/lbl declarations from C-like prototypes and maps func_/lbl_ addresses to a module prefix.
replace_symbol escapes slashes and dots in the search string and uses sed word boundaries to avoid accidental partial replacements.
rename_tu renames matching .c, .s, and .h files under asm/src/docs and then sed-updates references to the old basename plus extension.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in this PR slice.
The body only states: "Misc. QoL from #766." Any detailed motivation or reviewer intent likely lives in PR #766 rather than this PR.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #766 for the original context and motivation behind these tooling changes.
Search later PRs for tools/replace-module usage to see how module-prefix replacement was applied in real decomp submissions.
Search for follow-up changes to docs/glossary.md around SV, mv, MotionState, and ActionState naming.
Check later changes to compile_flags.txt or tools/issues to confirm the practical effect of -fno-short-enums on clang diagnostics.
Look for later edits to tools/functions.sh rename_tu or replace_symbol for refinements to bulk rename workflows.
